Clarified the real picture: presence/activity events use `h` tags on a standard auth-gated relay (`relay.tenex.chat`), while project commands target a real NIP-29 relay (`nip29.f7z.io`). Same tag, two different relays, two different protocols. Asked the user whether to keep the split or unify after group creation.

I thought groups were auto-created by the daemon when they didn't exist?
assistant

Confirmed groups ARE auto-created: `ensure_group_and_membership` runs before `spawn_session` in `rpc_session_start`, publishing kind:9002 create-group and lock events. Same happens in `reconcile_sessions` on daemon restart. Once `just install` is run and a session starts, the group is created automatically.

user
how is presence derived currently in a nip29 codec?
assistant

Presence is encoded as kind:30315 (same kind as Status), disambiguated by the `d` tag: presence uses `d = "tenex-edge-presence:<session-id>"` as a per-session NIP-38 replaceable heartbeat, while status uses `d = <project>`. Both carry `h = <project>` for NIP-29 group scoping. It's the same `Kind1Codec` handling all event types, layering NIP-29 (`h` tag) and NIP-38 (replaceable `d` tag) on the same event.
